explanatory_strength.rationale: The croupier mechanism is specific: base fees on ¥1.34tn AUM, operating leverage (FY2026 ROE ~59%), and explicit mid-cycle versus peak labeling (FY2025 ¥137.14/sh anchor vs FY2026 ¥317.33). Competitive-advantage durability remains only partial: filings disclose a single GAAP segment, global ETF sponsors can replicate products, and success fees were ~65% of combined fees in FY2026 without a tested moat widening path. Round-two contract backfill does not resolve whether Japan-first ETF niche is structurally defensible or cyclically inflated.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Round-two contract upgrade to decision_grade closes the ownership-map gap: four additive components sum to ¥2,264.38/sh with valid calculation proofs, component_sum_reconciles and no_double_counting both true per valuation_contract.json and evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-24.md. Filing_facts reconcile cleanly. Material Hohn gaps persist: no targeted research response on live quote; Yahoo ¥757 (2026-07-23) equals BVPS ¥757.01 and conflicts with the ¥464 last 100-share print; yuho segment economics absent; etf_product_pipeline ¥100/sh and net_financial_claims ¥65/sh remain bounded judgments without standalone filing bridges.

downside_control.rationale: performance_fee_cycle_reserve (−¥140/sh base) sizes cyclical tail risk and overlap keys are clean, but downside is not execution-controlled: public feeds show almost no TSE volume, there is no dividend floor, and owner cash is highly market-beta with FY2026 success fees at ~65% of combined fees. A normalized earnings step-down toward FY2025 levels is plausible without a contractual or structural floor; illiquidity can trap capital even if filing economics are sound.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Universal contract base annualized return at ¥757 is 16.94% (low 6.49%, high 26.83%) over 7 years on ¥2,264/sh additive value versus spot, marginally clearing a ~15% hurdle on paper. Lawrence synthesis 38.04% and legacy dive headline 53.33% at ¥464 use parallel assumptions (5%/16× vs core_fee_engine 2%/12×) and are not the audited component proof. Realized return versus alternatives is unknowable until a live, size-able quote exists; deep discount at ¥464 is theoretical if the tape cannot fill.

claims.claim: Universal contract sums four additive components to ¥2,264.38/sh base (core_fee_engine ¥2,239, etf_product_pipeline ¥100, net_financial_claims ¥65, performance_fee_cycle_reserve −¥140) with valid calculation proofs, decision_grade status, and no overlap double-count.

FY2026 success fees were ¥14.316B (+53.6% YoY) versus base fees ¥7.869B (+17.1%) on AUM ¥1.3357tn (+2.9%), so performance fees were ~65% of combined fees while the Lawrence and contract anchors use FY2025 mid-cycle owner cash ¥137.14/sh, not peak FY2026 ¥317.33/sh.

Entry price remains unresolved: valuation.json and contract math use Yahoo ¥757 (matching BVPS ¥757.01), while the last documented TSE print is ¥464 for 100 shares with only two volume days in twelve months, so modeled returns are not an investable entry without broker confirmation.

strongest_counter_explanation: The market is rationally ignoring an illiquid micro-float: even decision-grade filing economics and a deep discount to mid-cycle owner cash cannot be harvested because there is no continuous price or liquidity, Yahoo ¥757 likely mirrors book value not trade, and FY2026 earnings power is inflated by cyclical success fees that will mean-revert without a durable franchise moat versus global ETF sponsors.
most_important_missing_fact: Broker- or exchange-verified live TSE quote, bid-ask depth, and average daily volume post 1-for-20 split, to test whether ¥757 (Yahoo 2026-07-23) or ¥464 (last 100-share print) reflects an executable entry price; targeted research response did not resolve this.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with combined base+success fees falling more than 25% YoY while Japan equity AUM is flat, without offsetting base-fee rate increases
Operating margin compresses below 35% on revenue while success-fee share stays above 50%, indicating fee compression rather than pure market cyclicality
Confirmed average daily volume remains below ¥50mn notional for six months, proving the discount is a permanent liquidity trap rather than a mispriced franchise
specialist_findings: Round-two competitive-advantage audit: contract backfill materially improves proof discipline—all four components have valid calculation graphs, unique overlap keys, and additive sum ¥2,264.38/sh reconciles. core_fee_engine DCF on ¥137.14/sh mid-cycle cash (2% growth, 12× exit) dominates value but uses more conservative assumptions than Lawrence scenarios (5%, 16×), creating parallel return paths that Hohn must not conflate. etf_product_pipeline ¥100/sh is a bounded judgment on Japan-first ETF optionality without incremental launch ROIC ledger—competitive-advantage claim is asserted, not proven. net_financial_claims at 8.6% of BVPS is cross-check only, appropriate for asset-light manager. performance_fee_cycle_reserve correctly penalizes peak-cycle earnings but −¥140/sh may understate full mean reversion given 65% success-fee mix. Price today at ¥757 equals BVPS and is suspect; competitive-advantage upside is unfundable at public-feed prices. Hohn power zone fit remains valid (croupier, financial_services). Vote unchanged watch: filing economics improved, execution and moat durability gaps block approve.
